assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a foreign-currency monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation, so the item can qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements.
An eligible hedging instrument exists and the formal designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements are met.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognized intragroup dividend receivable can be treated as a hedged monetary item for foreign currency risk in consolidation when that risk affects consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also allows a recognized asset or liability to be designated in a cash flow hedge when the hedged foreign currency risk creates variability in cash flows that affects consolidated profit or loss.
